_comment: Plainfield Fire Department (PFD) agency config for the pediatric assessment SCORM pilot. Equipment and SOPs should remain in parity with the pilot Postgres agency config before packaging.

provider_levels.notes: PFD operates as BLS first-response; ALS provided by co-dispatched Medic unit.

service_type.notes: Non-transport BLS fire department. ALS is co-dispatched on all calls via WMRMCC. Transport is provided by Life Ambulance or Rockford Ambulance units. PFD provides first response - stabilize and prepare for ALS/transport.

als_dispatch.notes: ALS (Medic) is co-dispatched automatically to all Priority 1 calls per PFD dispatch protocol.

completed: Draw-up epinephrine IM - WMRMCC MCA-approved training completed; BLS personnel certified to draw up and administer epinephrine 1 mg/mL IM for anaphylaxis per Protocol 1-6
Albuterol SVN administration - EMT scope at WMRMCC
Naloxone IN administration - EMT scope per WMRMCC
I-Gel Supraglottic airway - per WMRMCC BLS airway protocol
Glucometer use - can obtain blood glucose/sugar level

sops.title: Non-Transport Operations
sops.text: PFD units do not transport patients. On all medical calls, ALS is co-dispatched via WMRMCC. Squad 1 initiates care, stabilizes the patient, and performs DMIST handoff to the arriving ALS crew. Document all interventions, times, and patient response for ALS continuity of care.

sops.title: Epinephrine Draw-Up - Anaphylaxis
sops.text: PFD units do NOT carry epinephrine auto-injectors. Draw-up epinephrine (1 mg/mL IM) is the only epinephrine option on this unit and is approved under WMRMCC Protocol 1-6 for BLS use after MCA-approved training. All personnel must complete the WMRMCC 9-1(S) two-step cross-check: verbalize to partner before drawing up AND before injecting. Weight-based dosing: 0.15 mg (0.15 mL) for 10-30 kg; 0.3 mg (0.3 mL) for > 30 kg / adults. Contact Medical Control for patients < 10 kg prior to administration if possible.

sops.title: Blood Glucose Testing
sops.text: Blood glucose check is an MFR and EMT/BLS skill at WMRMCC. EMT may administer oral glucose based on clinical presentation and protocol indications without a confirmed BG value if hypoglycemia is strongly suspected.

sops.title: ALS Handoff (DMIST)
sops.text: All patient contacts require a DMIST handoff to the arriving ALS crew: Demographics (name, age, weight), Mechanism/Complaint, Interventions performed (drug, dose, time, response), Signs (current vitals, trends), Transfer (relevant history, medications, allergies). Document all interventions on the ePCR.

sops.title: Pediatric Weight Estimation
sops.text: Use Broselow tape for all pediatric patients under 35 kg. Color band determines weight range for medication dosing. For oral glucose and draw-up epinephrine: dose is based on weight range, not exact weight. Always verbalize Broselow color and estimated weight to partner as part of medication cross-check.

ai_prompt_context: This is a Plainfield Fire Department (PFD) unit - a non-transport EMT-Basic BLS fire department first response unit. ALS is always co-dispatched and provides transport. PFD does NOT carry epinephrine auto-injectors; draw-up epinephrine IM (1 mg/mL) is the only epinephrine option on this unit, and personnel are trained and certified to use it per WMRMCC Protocol 1-6. Blood glucose testing is MFR and EMT scope at WMRMCC. All medication administration requires the WMRMCC 9-1(S) two-step cross-check before administering.
